methods.name: handleCustomPayload
methods.doc: Handles packets that have room for a channel specification. Vanilla implemented channels are "MC|TrList" to acquire a MerchantRecipeList trades for a villager merchant, "MC|Brand" which sets the server brand? on the player instance and finally "MC|RPack" which the server uses to communicate the identifier of the default server resourcepack for the client to load.

methods.name: createCropDrops
methods.doc:
If {@code dropGrownCropCondition} fails (i.e. crop is not ready), drops 1 {@code seedsItem}.
If {@code dropGrownCropCondition} succeeds (i.e. crop is ready), drops 1 {@code grownCropItem}, and 0-3 {@code seedsItem} with fortune applied.

methods.name: createLeavesDrops
methods.doc: Used for all leaves, drops self with silk touch, otherwise drops the second Block param with the passed chances for fortune levels, adding in sticks.

methods.name: createMushroomBlockDrop
methods.doc: Creates a builder that drops the given IItemProvider in amounts between 0 and 2, most often 0. Only used in vanilla for huge mushroom blocks.

methods.name: createOakLeavesDrops
methods.doc: Used for oak and dark oak, same as droppingWithChancesAndSticks but adding in apples.

methods.name: createSelfDropDispatchTable
methods.doc:
If the condition from {@code conditionBuilder} succeeds, drops 1 {@code block}.
Otherwise, drops loot specified by {@code alternativeEntryBuilder}.

methods.name: createShearsDispatchTable
methods.doc:
If the block is mined with Shears, drops 1 {@code block}.
Otherwise, drops loot specified by {@code alternativeEntryBuilder}.

methods.name: createSilkTouchDispatchTable
methods.doc:
If the block is mined with Silk Touch, drops 1 {@code block}.
Otherwise, drops loot specified by {@code alternativeEntryBuilder}.

methods.name: createSilkTouchOrShearsDispatchTable
methods.doc:
If the block is mined either with Silk Touch or Shears, drops 1 {@code block}.
Otherwise, drops loot specified by {@code alternativeEntryBuilder}.

methods.name: createStemDrops
methods.doc: Creates a builder that drops the given IItemProvider in amounts between 0 and 3, based on the AGE property. Only used in vanilla for pumpkin and melon stems.

methods.name: connectToLocalServer
methods.doc:
Prepares a clientside Connection for a local in-memory connection ("single player").
Establishes a connection to the socket supplied and configures the channel pipeline (only the packet handler is necessary,
since this is for an in-memory connection). Returns the newly created instance.

methods.name: disconnect
methods.doc:
Closes the channel with a given reason. The reason is stored for later and will be used for informational purposes (info log on server,
disconnection screen on the client). This method is also called on the client when the server requests disconnection via
{@code ClientboundDisconnectPacket}.

Closing the channel this way does not send any disconnection packets, it simply terminates the underlying netty channel.

methods.name: flushQueue
methods.doc: Will iterate through the outboundPacketQueue and dispatch all Packets

methods.name: handleDisconnection
methods.doc:
Checks if the channle is no longer active and if so, processes the disconnection
by notifying the current packet listener, which will handle things like removing the player from the world (serverside) or
showing the disconnection screen (clientside).

methods.name: isMemoryConnection
methods.doc: True if this NetworkManager uses a memory connection (single player game). False may imply both an active TCP connection or simply no active connection at all

fields: 1813:HANDSHAKING:The handshake protocol. This is the initial protocol, in which the client tells the server its intention (i.e. which protocol it wants to use).

1813:LOGIN:The login protocol. This is the first protocol the client switches to to join a server. It handles authentication with the mojang servers. After it is complete, the connection is switched to the PLAY protocol.
1813:PLAY:The play protocol. This is the main protocol that is used while "in game" and most normal packets reside in here.
1813:STATUS:The status protocol. This protocol is used when a client pings a server while on the multiplayer screen.
classes.doc:
Describes the set of packets a connection understands at a given point.
A connection always starts out in state {@link #HANDSHAKING}. In this state the client sends its desired protocol using
{@link ClientIntentionPacket}. The server then either accepts the connection and switches to the desired protocol or it disconnects the client (for example in case of an outdated client).

Each protocol has a {@link PacketListener} implementation tied to it for server and client respectively.

Every packet must correspond to exactly one protocol.

methods.name: writeVarInt
methods.doc: Writes a compressed int to the buffer. The smallest number of bytes to fit the passed int will be written. Of each such byte only 7 bits will be used to describe the actual value since its most significant bit dictates whether the next byte is part of that same int. Micro-optimization for int values that are usually small.

Variant of {@link Connection} that monitors the amount of received packets and disables receiving if the set limit is exceeded.

Used to signify that a packet encoding error is not fatal. If a packet fails to encode, but {@link Packet#isSkippable} returns true, then this exception is thrown instead and {@link Connection} will log a message instead of closing the connection.
Counterpart to {@link Varint21LengthFieldPrepender}. Decodes each frame ("packet") by first reading its length and then its data.

Prepends each frame ("packet") with its length encoded as a VarInt. Every frame's length must fit within a 3-byte VarInt.

@see Varint21FrameDecoder

If this component's style is currently null, it will be initialized to the default style, and the parent style of all sibling components will be set to that style. (IE, changes to this style will also be reflected in sibling components.) This method never returns null.

methods.name: ensureRunningOnSameThread
methods.doc:
Ensures that the given packet is handled on the main thread. If the current thread is not the main thread, this method
throws {@link RunningOnDifferentThreadException}, which is caught and ignored in the outer call ({@link Connection#channelRead0}). Additionally it then re-schedules the packet to be handled on the main thread,
which will then end up back here, but this time on the main thread.

which will then end up back here, but this time on the main thread.

methods.name: handleAddEntity
methods.doc: Spawns an instance of the objecttype indicated by the packet and sets its position and momentum

methods.name: handleAddExperienceOrb
methods.doc: Spawns an experience orb and sets its value (amount of XP)

methods.name: handleAddMob
methods.doc: Spawns the mob entity at the specified location, with the specified rotation, momentum and type. Updates the entities Datawatchers with the entity metadata specified in the packet

methods.name: handleAddObjective
methods.doc: May create a scoreboard objective, remove an objective from the scoreboard or update an objectives' displayname

methods.name: handleAddPainting
methods.doc: Handles the spawning of a painting object

methods.name: handleAddPlayer
methods.doc: Handles the creation of a nearby player entity, sets the position and held item

methods.name: handleAnimate
methods.doc: Renders a specified animation: Waking up a player, a living entity swinging its currently held item, being hurt or receiving a critical hit by normal or magical means

methods.name: handleAwardStats
methods.doc: Updates the players statistics or achievements

methods.name: handleBlockDestruction
methods.doc: Updates all registered IWorldAccess instances with destroyBlockInWorldPartially

methods.name: handleBlockEntityData
methods.doc: Updates the NBTTagCompound metadata of instances of the following entitytypes: Mob spawners, command blocks, beacons, skulls, flowerpot

methods.name: handleBlockEvent
methods.doc: Triggers Block.onBlockEventReceived, which is implemented in BlockPistonBase for extension/retraction, BlockNote for setting the instrument (including audiovisual feedback) and in BlockContainer to set the number of players accessing a (Ender)Chest

methods.name: handleBlockUpdate
methods.doc: Updates the block and metadata and generates a blockupdate (and notify the clients)

methods.name: handleChat
methods.doc: Prints a chatmessage in the chat GUI

methods.name: handleChunkBlocksUpdate
methods.doc: Received from the servers PlayerManager if between 1 and 64 blocks in a chunk are changed. If only one block requires an update, the server sends S23PacketBlockChange and if 64 or more blocks are changed, the server sends S21PacketChunkData

methods.name: handleCommandSuggestions
methods.doc: This method is only called for manual tab-completion (the {@link net.minecraft.command.arguments.SuggestionProviders#ASK_SERVER minecraft:ask_server} suggestion provider).

methods.name: handleContainerClose
methods.doc: Resets the ItemStack held in hand and closes the window that is opened

methods.name: handleContainerContent
methods.doc: Handles the placement of a specified ItemStack in a specified container/inventory slot

methods.name: handleContainerSetData
methods.doc: Sets the progressbar of the opened window to the specified value

methods.name: handleContainerSetSlot
methods.doc: Handles pickin up an ItemStack or dropping one in your inventory or an open (non-creative) container

methods.name: handleCustomPayload
methods.doc: Handles packets that have room for a channel specification. Vanilla implemented channels are "MC|TrList" to acquire a MerchantRecipeList trades for a villager merchant, "MC|Brand" which sets the server brand? on the player instance and finally "MC|RPack" which the server uses to communicate the identifier of the default server resourcepack for the client to load.

methods.name: handleDisconnect
methods.doc: Closes the network channel

methods.name: handleEntityEvent
methods.doc: Invokes the entities' handleUpdateHealth method which is implemented in LivingBase (hurt/death), MinecartMobSpawner (spawn delay), FireworkRocket & MinecartTNT (explosion), IronGolem (throwing,...), Witch (spawn particles), Zombie (villager transformation), Animal (breeding mode particles), Horse (breeding/smoke particles), Sheep (...), Tameable (...), Villager (particles for breeding mode, angry and happy), Wolf (...)

methods.name: handleExplosion
methods.doc: Initiates a new explosion (sound, particles, drop spawn) for the affected blocks indicated by the packet.

methods.name: handleLevelChunk
methods.doc: Updates the specified chunk with the supplied data, marks it for re-rendering and lighting recalculation

methods.name: handleLogin
methods.doc: Registers some server properties (gametype,hardcore-mode,terraintype,difficulty,player limit), creates a new WorldClient and sets the player initial dimension

methods.name: handleMapItemData
methods.doc: Updates the worlds MapStorage with the specified MapData for the specified map-identifier and invokes a MapItemRenderer for it

methods.name: handleMoveEntity
methods.doc: Updates the specified entity's position by the specified relative moment and absolute rotation. Note that subclassing of the packet allows for the specification of a subset of this data (e.g. only rel. position, abs. rotation or both).

methods.name: handleOpenSignEditor
methods.doc: Creates a sign in the specified location if it didn't exist and opens the GUI to edit its text

methods.name: handleParticleEvent
methods.doc: Spawns a specified number of particles at the specified location with a randomized displacement according to specified bounds

methods.name: handleRotateMob
methods.doc: Updates the direction in which the specified entity is looking, normally this head rotation is independent of the rotation of the entity itself

methods.name: handleSetCarriedItem
methods.doc: Updates which hotbar slot of the player is currently selected

methods.name: handleSetDisplayObjective
methods.doc: Removes or sets the ScoreObjective to be displayed at a particular scoreboard position (list, sidebar, below name)

methods.name: handleSetEntityData
methods.doc: Invoked when the server registers new proximate objects in your watchlist or when objects in your watchlist have changed -> Registers any changes locally

methods.name: handleSetEntityMotion
methods.doc: Sets the velocity of the specified entity to the specified value

methods.name: handleSetPlayerTeamPacket
methods.doc: Updates a team managed by the scoreboard: Create/Remove the team registration, Register/Remove the player-team-memberships, Set team displayname/prefix/suffix and/or whether friendly fire is enabled

methods.name: handleSetScore
methods.doc: Either updates the score with a specified value or removes the score for an objective

methods.name: handleTeleportEntity
methods.doc: Updates an entity's position and rotation as specified by the packet

methods.name: handleUpdateAttributes
methods.doc: Updates en entity's attributes and their respective modifiers, which are used for speed bonusses (player sprinting, animals fleeing, baby speed), weapon/tool attackDamage, hostiles followRange randomization, zombie maxHealth and knockback resistance as well as reinforcement spawning chance.

methods.name: handleChat
methods.doc: Process chat messages (broadcast back to clients) and commands (executes)

methods.name: handleClientCommand
methods.doc: Processes the client status updates: respawn attempt from player, opening statistics or achievements, or acquiring 'open inventory' achievement

methods.name: handleClientInformation
methods.doc: Updates serverside copy of client settings: language, render distance, chat visibility, chat colours, difficulty, and whether to show the cape

methods.name: handleContainerButtonClick
methods.doc: Enchants the item identified by the packet given some convoluted conditions (matching window, which should/shouldn't be in use?)

methods.name: handleContainerClick
methods.doc: Executes a container/inventory slot manipulation as indicated by the packet. Sends the serverside result if they didn't match the indicated result and prevents further manipulation by the player until he confirms that it has the same open container/inventory

methods.name: handleContainerClose
methods.doc: Processes the client closing windows (container)

methods.name: handleCustomCommandSuggestions
methods.doc: This method is only called for manual tab-completion (the {@link net.minecraft.command.arguments.SuggestionProviders#ASK_SERVER minecraft:ask_server} suggestion provider).

methods.name: handleCustomPayload
methods.doc: Synchronizes serverside and clientside book contents and signing

methods.name: handleInteract
methods.doc: Processes left and right clicks on entities

methods.name: handleKeepAlive
methods.doc: Updates a players' ping statistics

methods.name: handleMovePlayer
methods.doc: Processes clients perspective on player positioning and/or orientation

methods.name: handlePlayerAbilities
methods.doc: Processes a player starting/stopping flying

methods.name: handlePlayerAction
methods.doc: Processes the player initiating/stopping digging on a particular spot, as well as a player dropping items

methods.name: handlePlayerCommand
methods.doc: Processes a range of action-types: sneaking, sprinting, waking from sleep, opening the inventory or setting jump height of the horse the player is riding

methods.name: handlePlayerInput
methods.doc: Processes player movement input. Includes walking, strafing, jumping, sneaking" excludes riding and toggling flying/sprinting

methods.name: handleSetCarriedItem
methods.doc: Updates which quickbar slot is selected

methods.name: handleSetCreativeModeSlot
methods.doc: Update the server with an ItemStack in a slot.

methods.name: handleUseItem
methods.doc: Called when a client is using an item while not pointing at a block, but simply using an item

classes.doc:
Keeps data in sync from server to client for an entity.
A maximum of 254 parameters per entity class can be registered. The system then ensures that these values are updated on the client whenever they change on the server.

Use {@link #defineId} to register a piece of data for your entity class.
Use {@link #define} during {@link Entity#defineSynchedData} to set the default value for a given parameter.

fields: 2463:ARMOR:Handles the armor points for an entity. Each point represents half a chestplate of armor on the armor bar.
2463:ARMOR_TOUGHNESS:Handles the amount of damage mitigated by wearing armor.
2463:ATTACK_DAMAGE:Handles the attack damage inflicted by entities. The value of this attribute represents half hearts.
2463:ATTACK_KNOCKBACK:Handles additional horizontal knockback when damaging another entity.
2463:ATTACK_SPEED:Handles the cooldown rate when attacking with an item. The value represents the number of full strength attacks that can be performed per second.
2463:FLYING_SPEED:Handles the movement speed of flying entities such as parrots and bees.
2463:FOLLOW_RANGE:Handles the range in blocks that a mob will notice and track players and other potential targets.
2463:JUMP_STRENGTH:Handles the jump strength for horses.
2463:KNOCKBACK_RESISTANCE:Handles the reduction of horizontal knockback when damaged by attacks or projectiles.
2463:LUCK:Handles luck when a player generates loot from a loot table. This can impact the quality of loot and influence bonus rolls.
2463:MAX_HEALTH:Handles the maximum health of an entity.
2463:MOVEMENT_SPEED:Handles the movement speed of entities.
2463:SPAWN_REINFORCEMENTS_CHANCE:Handles the chance for a zombie to summon reinforcements when attacked.
classes.doc: Contains all entity attributes defined and registered by the vanilla game.

methods.name: sanitizeValue
desc: 2:value:The value of the attribute to sanitize.
methods.doc:
Sanitizes the value of the attribute to fit within the expected parameter range of the attribute. In this case it will clamp the value between {@link minValue} and {@link maxValue}.
@return The clamped attribute value.

fields: 2:maxValue:The highest possible value for the attribute.
2:minValue:The lowest possible value for the attribute.

methods.name: findPath
methods.doc:
Find and return a path among the circles described by pathPoints, or null if the shortest path would just be directly between the start and finish with no intermediate points.

Starting with pathPoint[startIdx], it searches the neighboring points (and their neighboring points, and so on) until it reaches pathPoint[finishIdx], at which point it calls makePath to seal the deal.

methods.name: onlyOpCanSetNbt
methods.doc:
Checks if players can use this entity to access operator (permission level 2) commands either directly or indirectly, such as give or setblock. A similar method exists for entities at {@link net.minecraft.tileentity.TileEntity#onlyOpsCanSetNbt()}.

For example, {@link net.minecraft.entity.item.EntityMinecartCommandBlock#ignoreItemEntityData() command block minecarts} and {@link net.minecraft.entity.item.EntityMinecartMobSpawner#ignoreItemEntityData() mob spawner minecarts} (spawning command block minecarts or drops) are considered accessible.

@return true if this entity offers ways for unauthorized players to use restricted commands

Checks if players can use this entity to access operator (permission level 2) commands either directly or indirectly, such as give or setblock. A similar method exists for entities at {@link net.minecraft.tileentity.TileEntity#onlyOpsCanSetNbt()}.

For example, {@link net.minecraft.entity.item.EntityMinecartCommandBlock#ignoreItemEntityData() command block minecarts} and {@link net.minecraft.entity.item.EntityMinecartMobSpawner#ignoreItemEntityData() mob spawner minecarts} (spawning command block minecarts or drops) are considered accessible.

@return true if this entity offers ways for unauthorized players to use restricted commands

methods.name: isFoil
methods.doc: Returns true if this item has an enchantment glint. By default, this returns stack.isItemEnchanted(), but other items can override it (for instance, written books always return true). Note that if you override this method, you generally want to also call the super version (on {@link Item}) to get the glint for enchanted items. Of course, that is unnecessary if the overwritten version always returns true.

Note that if you override this method, you generally want to also call the super version (on {@link Item}) to get the glint for enchanted items. Of course, that is unnecessary if the overwritten version always returns true.

methods.name: byName
desc: 331:name:The name of the potion to search for. This name will be parsed as a namespaced identifier.
methods.doc:
Attempts to find a Potion using a name. The name will be parsed as a namespaced identifier which will be used to lookup the potion in the potion registry.
@return The potion that was found in the registry.

methods.name: getEffects
methods.doc:
Gets the base effects applied by the potion.
@return The effects applied by the potion.

methods.name: getName
desc: 331:prefix:The prefix to add on to the base name.
methods.doc:
Gets the prefixed potion name. This is often used to create a localization key for items like the tipped arrows or bottled potion.
@return The prefixed potion name.

methods.name: hasInstantEffects
methods.doc:
Checks if the potion contains any instant effects such as instant health or instant damage.
@return Whether or not the potion contained an instant effect.

methods.name: isCurse
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment is considered a curse. These enchantments are treated as debuffs and can not be removed from items under normal circumstances.
@return Whether or not the enchantment is a curse.

methods.name: isTreasureOnly
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment should be considered a treasure enchantment. These enchantments can not be obtained using the enchantment table. The mending enchantment is an example of a treasure enchantment.
@return Whether or not the enchantment is a treasure enchantment.

methods.name: shouldIgnoreDurabilityDrop
methods.doc: Used by ItemStack.attemptDamageItem. Randomly determines if a point of damage should be negated using the enchantment level (par1). If the ItemStack is Armor then there is a flat 60% chance for damage to be negated no matter the enchantment level, otherwise there is a 1-(par/1) chance for damage to be negated.

methods.doc: Gets an enchantment from the registry using it's internal numeric Id.

methods.name: canEnchant
methods.doc: Checks if the enchantment can be applied to a given ItemStack.

methods.name: checkCompatibility
desc: 3274:other:The other enchantment to test compatibility with.
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment can be applied with another enchantment. Being incompatible will prevent the two enchantments from being applied to the same item.
@return Whether or not the two enchantments are compatible.

methods.name: doPostAttack
methods.doc: A hook that is called every time an entity with this enchantment attacks another entity.

methods.name: doPostHurt
methods.doc: A hook that is called every time an entity with this enchantment is attacked by another entity.

methods.name: getDamageBonus
desc: 4:level:The level of the enchantment being used.
methods.doc: Calculates the amount of additional damage to deal when attacking with an item with the enchantment.

methods.name: getDamageProtection
desc: 4:level:The level of the enchantment being used.
methods.doc: Calculates the amount of additional damage protection to provide when a user is damaged.

methods.name: getDescriptionId
methods.doc: Gets the description Id of the enchantment. This is commonly used to create localization keys.

methods.name: getFullname
methods.doc:
Gets the name of the enchantment with the level appended to the end. The game will attempt to convert the letter to roman numerals.
@return The name of the enchantment with the level appened to the end.

methods.name: getMaxLevel
methods.doc: Gets the maximum level of the enchantment under normal circumstances such as the enchanting table. This limit is not strictly enforced and may be ignored through custom item NBT or other customizations.

methods.name: getMinCost
methods.doc: Returns the minimal value of enchantability needed on the enchantment level passed.

methods.name: getMinLevel
methods.doc: Gets the minimum level of the enchantment under normal circumstances such as the enchanting table. This limit is not strictly enforced and may be ignored through custom item NBT or other customizations.

methods.name: getOrCreateDescriptionId
methods.doc: Lazily initializes a descriptionId for an enchantment using the enchantments registry name.

methods.name: getRarity
methods.doc: Gets the rarity of the enchantment. This determines how rare the enchantment will be in enchantment pools such as the enchanting table and loot tables.

methods.name: getSlotItems
methods.doc: Creates a new map containing all items equipped by an entity in {@linkplain #slots slots that the enchantment cares about}. These items are not tested for having the enchantment.

methods.name: isCompatibleWith
desc: 3274:other:The other enchantment to test compatibility with.
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment is compatible with another enchantment.
@return Whether or not both enchantments agree that they are compatible with eachother.

methods.name: isCurse
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment is considered a curse. These enchantments are treated as debuffs and can not be removed from items under normal circumstances.
@return Whether or not the enchantment is a curse.

methods.name: isDiscoverable
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment can be discovered by game mechanics which pull random enchanments from the enchantment registry.
@return Whether or not the enchantment can be discovered.

methods.name: isTradeable
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment can be traded by NPCs like villagers.
@return Whether or not this enchantment can be traded.

methods.name: isTreasureOnly
methods.doc:
Checks if the enchantment should be considered a treasure enchantment. These enchantments can not be obtained using the enchantment table. The mending enchantment is an example of a treasure enchantment.
@return Whether or not the enchantment is a treasure enchantment.

methods.doc: Plays a sound. On the server, the sound is broadcast to all nearby except the given player. On the client, the sound only plays if the given player is the client player. Thus, this method is intended to be called from code running on both sides. The client plays it locally and the server plays it for everyone else.

methods.name: setBlock
methods.doc:
Sets a block state into this world.Flags are as follows:
1 will cause a block update.
2 will send the change to clients.
4 will prevent the block from being re-rendered.
8 will force any re-renders to run on the main thread instead
16 will prevent neighbor reactions (e.g. fences connecting, observers pulsing).
32 will prevent neighbor reactions from spawning drops.
64 will signify the block is being moved.
Flags can be OR-ed

Update the provided state given the provided neighbor facing and neighbor state, returning a new state.
For example, fences make their connections to the passed in state if possible, and wet concrete powder immediately returns its solidified counterpart.
Note that this method should ideally consider only the specific face passed in.

methods.name: triggerEvent
methods.doc:
Called on server when World#addBlockEvent is called. If server returns true, then also called on the client. On the Server, this may perform additional changes to the world, like pistons replacing the block with an extended base. On the client, the update may involve replacing tile entities or effects such as sounds or particles
@deprecated call via {@link IBlockState#onBlockEventReceived(World,BlockPos,int,int)} whenever possible. Implementing/overriding is fine.

methods.name: onlyOpCanSetNbt
methods.doc:
Checks if players can use this tile entity to access operator (permission level 2) commands either directly or indirectly, such as give or setblock. A similar method exists for entities at {@link net.minecraft.entity.Entity#ignoreItemEntityData()}.

For example, {@link net.minecraft.tileentity.TileEntitySign#onlyOpsCanSetNbt() signs} (player right-clicking) and {@link net.minecraft.tileentity.TileEntityCommandBlock#onlyOpsCanSetNbt() command blocks} are considered accessible.

@return true if this block entity offers ways for unauthorized players to use restricted commands

methods.name: heightDependentRadiusEllipse
desc: 4:y:The y value to calculate a radius at.
4:maxRadius:The maximum radius of the iceberg, at the horizontal.
methods.doc:
Given a horizontal projection of an iceberg, defines the three dimensional extrusion by defining a radius at any given y value.
The radius curve is a parabolic function, resulting in more rounded iceberg peaks.

methods.name: heightDependentRadiusSteep
desc: 374:random:A random to use to vary the slope of the falloff curve.
4:y:The y value to calculate a radius at.
4:maxRadius:The maximum radius radius of the iceberg, at the horizontal.
methods.doc:
Given a horizontal projection of an iceberg, defines the three dimensional extrusion by defining a radius at any given y value.
The radius curve is a linear function, with a slope that is both dependent on the {@code majorAxis} and randomly varies, which results in steep conical icebergs.

methods.name: signedDistanceEllipse
desc: 4:x:The x position of the point to measure the distance to.
4:z:The z position of the point to measure distance to.
4:majorRadius:The semi-major axis (@code a}) of the ellipse.
4:minorRadius:The semi-minor axis ({@code b}) of the ellipse
2:angle:The rotation angle of the ellipse (the angle from the positive horizontal axis to the ellipse's major axis).
methods.doc:
Given an ellipse defined by the equation {@code (x/a)^2 + (y/b)^2 = 1}, where {@code a} and {@code b} are the semi-major and semi-minor axies respectively, this computes the distance between an arbitrary point and the ellipse.
The point (x, y) is within the ellipse if the return value is < 0, outside the ellipse if the return value is > 0 and exactly on the edge of the ellipse if the return value is 0.

methods.name: smooth

classes.doc:
This feature generates part of the icebergs found in frozen oceans.
Specifically, it generates tall, triangular prism icebergs, and "donut" or torus shaped icebergs.
Other icebergs are generated by the frozen ocean surface builder instead.

classes.doc: Creates Gson serializers based on {@link SerializerType} and {@link Serializer}. The resulting serializers handle JSON in the following structure: { "type": "minecraft:example", "otherProperties": "go here" } The {@code "type"} property is read (with a fallback to {@code defaultType}) and then looked up in the provided registry to produce the {@code SerializerType}. The type's {@code Serializer} is then used to deserialize the rest of the JSON object. If the serializer receives a JSON element that is not an object, it will try to use the {@code InlineSerializer} if provided.

LootContext stores various context information for loot generation.
This includes the Level as well as any known {@link LootContextParam}s.

methods.name: withDynamicDrop
methods.doc:
Registers a DynamicDrop to the LootContext.

@see LootContext.DynamicDrop

classes.doc:
DynamicDrop allows a loot generating object (e.g. a Block or Entity) to provide dynamic drops to a loot table.
An example of this are shulker boxes, which provide their contents as a dynamic drop source.
Dynamic drops are registered with a name using {@link LootContext.Builder#withDynamicDrop}.

These dynamic drops can then be referenced from a loot table using {@link DynamicLoot}.

methods.name: addRandomItems
methods.doc:
Generate the random items from this LootPool to the given {@code stackConsumer}.
This first checks this pool's conditions, generating nothing if they do not match.

methods.name: contains
methods.doc: Returns if the supplied Vec3D is completely inside the bounding box

methods.name: contract
methods.doc:
Creates a new {@link AxisAlignedBB} that has been contracted by the given amount, with positive changes decreasing max values and negative changes increasing min values.
If the amount to contract by is larger than the length of a side, then the side will wrap (still creating a valid AABB - see last sample).

Samples:

InputResult
new AxisAlignedBB(0, 0, 0, 4, 4, 4).contract(2, 2, 2)
box[0.0, 0.0, 0.0 -> 2.0, 2.0, 2.0]
new AxisAlignedBB(0, 0, 0, 4, 4, 4).contract(-2, -2, -2)
box[2.0, 2.0, 2.0 -> 4.0, 4.0, 4.0]
new AxisAlignedBB(5, 5, 5, 7, 7, 7).contract(0, 1, -1)
box[5.0, 5.0, 6.0 -> 7.0, 6.0, 7.0]
new AxisAlignedBB(-2, -2, -2, 2, 2, 2).contract(4, -4, 0)
box[-8.0, 2.0, -2.0 -> -2.0, 8.0, 2.0]

See Also:

  • {@link #expand(double, double, double)} - like this, except for expanding.
  • {@link #grow(double, double, double)} and {@link #grow(double)} - expands in all directions.
  • {@link #shrink(double)} - contracts in all directions (like {@link #grow(double)})
@return A new modified bounding box.

methods.name: deflate
methods.doc:
Creates a new {@link AxisAlignedBB} that is expanded by the given value in all directions. Equivalent to {@link #grow(double)} with value set to the negative of the value provided here. Passing a negative value to this method values will grow the AABB.
Side lengths will be decreased by 2 times the value of the parameter, since both min and max are changed.
If contracting and the amount to contract by is larger than the length of a side, then the side will wrap (still creating a valid AABB - see samples on {@link #grow(double, double, double)}). @return A modified AABB.

methods.name: expandTowards
methods.doc:
Creates a new {@link AxisAlignedBB} that has been expanded by the given amount, with positive changes increasing max values and negative changes decreasing min values.

Samples:

InputResult
new AxisAlignedBB(0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1).expand(2, 2, 2)
box[0, 0, 0 -> 3, 3, 3]
 
new AxisAlignedBB(0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1).expand(-2, -2, -2)
box[-2, -2, -2 -> 1, 1, 1]
 
new AxisAlignedBB(5, 5, 5, 7, 7, 7).expand(0, 1, -1)
box[5, 5, 4, 7, 8, 7]

See Also:

  • {@link #contract(double, double, double)} - like this, except for shrinking.
  • {@link #grow(double, double, double)} and {@link #grow(double)} - expands in all directions.
  • {@link #shrink(double)} - contracts in all directions (like {@link #grow(double)})
@return A modified bounding box that will always be equal or greater in volume to this bounding box.

methods.name: getSize
methods.doc: Returns the average length of the edges of the bounding box.

methods.name: inflate
methods.doc:
Creates a new {@link AxisAlignedBB} that is expanded by the given value in all directions. Equivalent to {@link #grow(double, double, double)} with the given value for all 3 params. Negative values will shrink the AABB.
Side lengths will be increased by 2 times the value of the parameter, since both min and max are changed.
If contracting and the amount to contract by is larger than the length of a side, then the side will wrap (still creating a valid AABB - see samples on {@link #grow(double, double, double)}). @return A modified AABB.

methods.name: inflate
methods.doc:
Creates a new {@link AxisAlignedBB} that has been contracted by the given amount in both directions. Negative values will shrink the AABB instead of expanding it.
Side lengths will be increased by 2 times the value of the parameters, since both min and max are changed.
If contracting and the amount to contract by is larger than the length of a side, then the side will wrap (still creating a valid AABB - see last ample).

Samples:

InputResult
new AxisAlignedBB(0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1).grow(2, 2, 2)
box[-2.0, -2.0, -2.0 -> 3.0, 3.0, 3.0]
new AxisAlignedBB(0, 0, 0, 6, 6, 6).grow(-2, -2, -2)
box[2.0, 2.0, 2.0 -> 4.0, 4.0, 4.0]
new AxisAlignedBB(5, 5, 5, 7, 7, 7).grow(0, 1, -1)
box[5.0, 4.0, 6.0 -> 7.0, 8.0, 6.0]
new AxisAlignedBB(1, 1, 1, 3, 3, 3).grow(-4, -2, -3)
box[-1.0, 1.0, 0.0 -> 5.0, 3.0, 4.0]

See Also:

  • {@link #expand(double, double, double)} - expands in only one direction.
  • {@link #contract(double, double, double)} - contracts in only one direction.
    • {@link #grow(double)} - version of this that expands in all directions from one parameter.
  • {@link #shrink(double)} - contracts in all directions
@return A modified bounding box.
